title: Dashboard: harden Leaflet rendering against NaN coords (issue #15)
description:
## Problem
GitHub issue #15 — Walter reports white screen + browser console "Uncaught Error: Invalid LatLng object: (NaN, NaN)" whenever the mower starts mowing on a stock v5.7.1 firmware. Server v2026.0429.1314.

Root cause: dashboard rendering assumes finite charger GPS + sensor latitude/longitude. On stock v5.x firmware with no charger calibration yet, [coords.ts](dashboard/src/utils/coords.ts) localToGps() multiplies through a non-finite chargerGps and propagates NaN into Polygon/Polyline positions; Leaflet rejects with a hard throw at construction time and React's render path white-screens.

## Fix landed
- [coords.ts](dashboard/src/utils/coords.ts) — added `isUsableChargerGps()` guard, polygonToLatLng skips non-finite vertices.
- [MowerMap.tsx](dashboard/src/components/map/MowerMap.tsx) — gpsMaps memo flatMap-filters NaN, position falls back to DEFAULT_CENTER on non-finite, polyCenter ignores non-finite vertices, pathDirectionPreview gates on Number.isFinite(polyCenter).
- [MiniMap.tsx](dashboard/src/mobile/components/MiniMap.tsx) — same guard pattern.

## Acceptance
- Walter's repro (open dashboard while mowing on stock v5.7.1, no charger calibration) renders the map without white-screen.
- No regression on properly calibrated rigs (live mowing on LFIN1231000211 still shows polygons + trail + heatmap).
- TS + vite build pass clean.

## Followup
- Bigger dashboard rebuild tracked under Novabot-08d.
close_reason: NaN guard shipped on feat/dashboard-catchup commit 1f3f2c7e (issue #15)

title: Phase 5 admin UI — needs browser smoke test
description:
## Why deferred
Plan calls for 3 admin-panel buttons that need browser verification on the running OpenNova instance with the Novabot stock app and OpenNova app actively connected (so toggling is_active visibly affects equipment listings).

## Remaining tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md Phase 5)
- Task 5.3 Recalibrate-charging-pose UI button (server endpoint already exists per commit 9640363f) + confirm modal warning that the mower will reboot.
- Task 5.4 is_active toggle UI (server endpoint /api/admin-status/set-active-device + /deactivate-device already exist).
- Task 5.5 mDNS soft-restart UI button — calls /api/admin-status/mdns-restart (shipped in commit 12da8895).

Task 5.1 (server is_active endpoint) was already covered by existing /api/admin-status/set-active-device — no new work was needed there.

## Acceptance
Resume after user verifies each button in browser triggers the expected MQTT/admin-side behavior on a running mower without breaking Novabot stock app or OpenNova app.
close_reason: mDNS restart button shipped (commit ef65f6cc). Activate + recalibrate buttons were already in place — see adminPage.ts:294 and :1233

title: Phase 4 drawer UI cards — needs browser smoke test
description:
## Why deferred
Drawer cards consume the three /api/dashboard/system/* endpoints (already shipped under feat/dashboard-catchup commits 01e8b90a, 7fee9ff6, e3b7a8bb). Cards are visual UI that need a live browser smoke test which the autonomous run could not perform.

## Remaining tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md Phase 4)
- Task 4.4 NetworkHealthCard — polls /api/dashboard/system/health every 30s, renders mDNS + broker uptime + per-mower last-seen pills.
- Task 4.5 LiveStatusCard — polls /api/dashboard/system/lora-status/:sn every 30s, renders LoRa pair + drift indicator + is_active flag.
- Task 4.6 ServerLogTail — fetches /api/dashboard/system/logs?tail=200, channel filter dropdown (all/connect/disconnect/publish/forward/error), refresh every 5s.
- Task 4.7 i18n keys for the drawer cards.
- Task 4.8 Phase 4 phase-merge gate (smoke test).

## Acceptance
Resume after user open drawer in browser, verify all three cards render data live, server log tail filters work end-to-end.
close_reason: Drawer cards shipped: NetworkHealthCard + LiveStatusCard + ServerLogTail in commit 413a679c

title: Phase 1 (Map) — deferred from autonomous run, needs user smoke testing
description:
## Why deferred
The dashboard catch-up plan's Phase 1 splits the 2000+ line \`MowerMap.tsx\` and adds 8 new map features (canonical-name render, obstacle fix, CuttingHeightPicker, EdgeCutButton, MapImportWizard, coverage progress, manual mowing, long-pause safety). All of these touch live Leaflet rendering that cannot be verified by TypeScript or vite build alone — they need browser smoke testing on a live mower.

The user gave autonomous authority for Phases 2–5 but explicitly asked for browser review after Phase 0. Phase 1's risk profile is too high to land blindly.

## What was done
- Phase 0 foundation shipped (DashboardShell + tabs + drawer + multi-mower context) on \`feat/dashboard-catchup\` ✅
- NaN guard for issue #15 shipped (closes Novabot-yp3) ✅
- Shell layout fix (h-screen flex column) shipped ✅

## Remaining Phase 1 tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md)
- Task 1.2 — Extract \`_shared.ts\` helpers from MowerMap.tsx
- Task 1.3 — Extract \`MapPolygonLayer\`
- Task 1.4 — Extract \`MapTrailLayer\`
- Task 1.5 — Extract \`CoverageLayer\` (used by both MowerMap and MiniMap)
- Task 1.6 — Extract \`PathDirectionPreview\`
- Task 1.7 — Extract \`MowerMarker\`
- Task 1.8 — Extract \`MapCanvas\` (final shell)
- Task 1.9 — Polygon canonical-name match render (port server commit 7b68b6a3 to dashboard rendering)
- Task 1.10 — PolygonEditor obstacle + canonical fix
- Task 1.11 — CuttingHeightPicker
- Task 1.12 — EdgeCutButton
- Task 1.13 — MapImportWizard
- Task 1.14 — Coverage progress visualization
- Task 1.15 — ManualMowControls
- Task 1.16 — Long-pause safety
- Task 1.17 — i18n keys for Phase 1
- Task 1.18 — Phase 1 phase-merge gate

## Acceptance
Resume after the user runs through the Phase 0 browser smoke test and confirms the new shell is OK to build on. Then dispatch implementer subagents per the plan, smoke test after each layer extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 implementer dispatches resumed under user supervision; map view continues to render polygons + trail + coverage stripes after each layer extraction.

title: Dashboard rebuild — sync with current app/server feature set
description:
## Background
Dashboard ([dashboard/](dashboard/)) has effectively been frozen since 2026-04-13. Only 4 commits in its lifetime (2026-04-06, 2026-04-09, 2026-04-13, 2026-04-28 — last is a one-line cosmetic charger-label fix). In the same window the app received 97 commits and the cloud-API tree picked up ~25 commits with new endpoints, new field shapes and behavior changes the dashboard does not reflect.

Issue #15 (Walter, 2026-04-29) is a symptom: rendering paths assume well-formed GPS data and crash with "Invalid LatLng object: (NaN, NaN)" the moment a stock-firmware mower with no charger calibration starts mowing. Hardened defensively in 1ec... follow-up commit but the underlying lag remains.

## Things missing from dashboard that landed elsewhere

- Coverage progress visualization, manual mowing, long-pause safety (e5ed8875)
- Schedule status display, rain warning, mower nickname (8ff753bd)
- mDNS auto-discovery surface (ab3a1833)
- Polygon edit-in-app + obstacle fix (a795c9a1)
- Cutting-height picker (11d8d410)
- Recalibrate charging pose (admin/app, 9640363f)
- Cloud-import merge mode + historic work records (63698470, a9992c63)
- Schedule fixes: alias resolve (b1182e78), week-day next-occurring (c644b29a), cutGrassHeight/area/timezone persistence (31da6323)
- Map polygon canonical-name CSV match (7b68b6a3)
- Work-records multipart parse + name-variant fallbacks (5d10add0, d8bc7b8a)
- Equipment is_active flag for multi-pair (e27c7107)
- LoRa pair drift fix surfacing (bef98c07)
- Notifications channel (5c54c901)

## Acceptance criteria

- New work plan (sub-issues) covering each feature gap
- Dashboard map renders cleanly when chargerGps is missing or sensor lat/lng feed NaN (regression test for issue #15)
- Schedule view shows the same fields the app shows (cutGrassHeight, area, timezone, next-occurring weekday, alias-resolved zone names)
- Map operations match app: polygon edit, obstacle add/remove, recalibrate charging pose, manual mowing, edge-cut trigger
- Work-records list reflects new server multipart + sensor-cache enrichment
- Multi-equipment users see is_active filtering
- Either a top-level rebuild epic or a series of beads sub-issues filed and dependency-linked

title: HomeScreen render all work polygons, not just one
description:
## Problem
Reported in https://github.com/rvbcrs/Novabot/issues/14 — when a schedule covers multiple maps, the OpenNova app home screen only renders ONE work polygon. If the mower is mowing map 2 or 3, but \`current_map_ids\` MQTT sensor is \`0\` / stale, the screen falls back to \`workMaps[0]\` and the mower icon appears OUTSIDE the visible polygon (icon plotted in global UTM frame, polygon is map 1/3).

## Location
\`app/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx:727-754\` — single-polygon \`setActiveMapPolygon(activeWork.mapArea)\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Home screen renders ALL \`mapType === 'work'\` polygons.
- The polygon matching \`current_map_ids - 1\` (or first when \`current_map_ids === 0\`) is highlighted (different stroke / fill).
- Obstacle polygons keep rendering for every work map (already in a separate state).
- Demo mode keeps a single demo polygon.
- Reporter from issue #14 confirms the mower icon now sits inside the correct polygon.

## Notes
- \`activeMapPolygon\` is consumed by \`MowerMapView\` further down the file — needs API tweak to accept an array of polygons + an active index.
- Compose-level test scenario: 3 work maps, mower currently on map index 2, current_map_ids=3 — verify highlight on third polygon and mower icon inside it.
acceptance_criteria: HomeScreen draws all 3 work polygons, active one highlighted, mower icon overlaps active polygon
